title: Guardian connector.yaml schema (v0.5.0)
description: Canonical schema for every connector.yaml file Guardian loads. Applies uniformly to bundle-shipped connectors (bundles/spark/connectors/<id>/connector.yaml) and operator-uploaded connectors (/app/data/user_connectors/<id>/connector.yaml). The MCP validates every connector.yaml against this schema at boot via usecase.connector_schema.validate_connector_spec; load fails fast on drift. Schema-by-example was the pre-v0.5.0 state and produced silent drift between connectors; this file codifies the shape so a typo or missing field surfaces at boot, not at first tool call. Tracks Spark v1.2 agent-bundle spec §7.4 — kept in sync as that spec evolves.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier — lives in the URL (/api/v1/connectors/<id>) and in tool names (<id>/<tool>). Must match the directory name the YAML lives in. Lowercase ASCII + digits + hyphens only.

version.description: SemVer. Bundle connectors track the agent image version (bumped together at release). User connectors version themselves.

description.description: One-line operator-facing description — shows on the marketplace card.

displayName.description: Optional human-readable name. Falls back to id.title() when absent.

kind.description: Marketplace entry kind (v0.2.42+). 'connector' (the implicit default when absent) is an integration the agent calls — it advertises ≥1 tool and is reached internally over the compose network. 'service' is an emulated service (e.g. the Splunk mimic): it advertises ZERO agent tools and instead publishes a host port (see `service.ports`) so an EXTERNAL system (e.g. an XSOAR server) connects to it as if it were the real product. connector_loader.iter_registrations() skips kind:service so the agent never gets a handle to a service — preserving the credential/catalog boundary. Optional + additive: omitting it means 'connector', so no existing connector.yaml changes meaning.

service.description: Service-kind runtime block (v0.2.42+). Only meaningful when `kind` is 'service'. Declares the host ports guardian-updater publishes for the service container so external systems can reach it (connectors are internal-only and never publish ports).

ports.description: Ports the service container publishes on the Docker host. Each entry maps a container port to a host port (defaults to the same number) over tcp/udp. guardian-updater passes these to client.containers.run(..., ports=...).

container_port.description: Port the service listens on inside the container (e.g. 8089 for splunkd).

host_port.description: Port published on the Docker host. Defaults to container_port when omitted.

protocol.description: Transport protocol for the published port.

image.description: Optional. OCI image reference for the per-instance container. Required for user-uploaded connectors (must point to a published image the host's docker can pull, e.g. 'ghcr.io/your-org/your-connector:v1.2'). Bundle connectors leave this empty — guardian-updater derives 'ghcr.io/<repo-owner>/guardian-connector-<id>:<version>' from the connector id and the running stack version. v0.5.0+ field; pre-v0.5.0 connectors had no per-YAML image declaration.

logo.description: Optional. Connector logo as a base64-encoded data URI (v0.5.52+). Supported MIME types: image/svg+xml, image/png, image/jpeg, image/gif, image/webp. Max length 350000 chars (≈260 KB encoded). Embedded in the YAML so the manifest round-trips on download/upload without a separate asset file. SVG is preferred for crispness at any size; PNG works fine for raster logos. The marketplace card renders this via when present, falling back to a generated placeholder when absent. Validate the encoded length client-side before upload so the operator sees the error immediately instead of after a multipart round-trip.

language.description: Implementation language. Currently only 'python' is supported; future runtimes (node, go) would extend this enum.

entrypoint.description: Module import path relative to the connector dir (e.g. 'src.connector'). Container-style: this is what the container's MCP server loads. Module-style (deprecated post-v0.5.0): what connector_loader imports in-process.

style.description: Dispatch model. v0.5.0+ supports ONLY 'container' — every connector runs as a per-instance guardian-connector-<id>-<name> container, dispatched over MCP-over-HTTP. The pre-v0.5.0 'module' (in-process import) and 'class' (in-process instantiate) styles were deleted as part of the universal container-mode migration; the schema rejects them so a user-uploaded YAML can't reintroduce them.

functionPrefix.description: Module-style legacy field — strips this prefix from underlying function names to produce tool names (e.g. 'guardian_' so guardian_create_data_worker becomes create_data_worker). Ignored when style: container.

executionMode.description: Optional. 'persistent' (default) keeps the connector process alive between tool calls. 'ephemeral' was reserved for one-shot connectors; no current bundle connector uses it. Omitted = persistent.

configSchema.description: JSON-Schema fragment describing the per-instance config the operator fills in on /connectors → Create Instance. The setup form is rendered directly from this — every property becomes a form field. Required[] gates submission. Default values pre-fill the form. v0.5.70+ optional `widget` keyword on each property selects the input widget (text | url | password | textarea | select | radio | multi_select | switch | array); when omitted, the widget is derived from the JSON-Schema `type`: string→text, integer/number→numeric text, boolean→switch, array→multi_select if `enum` present else array (chip list). Synthetic-card config blocks in mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts already use widget hints via the `type` field of each ConfigParam — the values match this taxonomy (text/url/password/secret/textarea/select/radio/multi_select/boolean/array) so the UI renderer (mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx → renderConfigField) can dispatch on `type` directly. See [help/architecture#connectors-design] for the widget table + a worked example.

secretSlots.description: Declared secret slots — per-instance encrypted values stored in the SecretStore. Empty array = connector has no secrets (e.g. cortex-docs reads public APIs).

name.description: Slot name. The setup form renders one secret field per slot; the value is encrypted at rest under GUARDIAN_SECRET_KEK; the connector reads it at call time via get_config().<name>.

description.description: Operator-facing description of what this secret is and where to find it.

required.description: When true, instance creation refuses if this slot is empty. When false, the connector handles the missing-secret case (e.g. xsiam's optional webhookKey).

tools.description: Declared tools the connector exposes. Each entry becomes a registered MCP tool when an instance of the connector is configured + the connector is installed via the marketplace. Connectors (kind:connector) MUST declare ≥1 tool — enforced in usecase.connector_schema.validate_connector_spec, not by minItems here, so that services (kind:service) may legitimately declare zero.

name.description: Tool name. The MCP exposes it as <connector_id>/<tool_name> (e.g. 'xsiam/run_xql_query').

method.description: Optional human-facing summary of the underlying call (e.g. 'GET /api/operations'). Documentation-only.

description.description: Operator-facing tool description — used by the agent's tool catalogue.

args.description: Tool parameters. Each becomes a typed argument in the generated MCP tool signature.

returns.description: Optional. Documents the shape of the tool's return value. Documentation-only — not enforced at dispatch.
